content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 19, 1988

Linda D. Hamilton
Secretary/Deputy County Clerk
Superior Court/County Clerk
County of San Bernardino
351 No. Arrowhead Ave., Fifth Floor
San Bernardino, CA 92415-0210
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-88-251

Dear Ms. Hamilton:

I am responding to your letter of June 27, 1988. You have
requested clarification of a previous advice letter (Hamilton
Advice Letter, No. A-88-141) regarding the officer with whom
copies of candidate statements of economic interests (Form 721)
are filed under the Political Reform Act.1/

QUESTION

Which county official is responsible for retaining copies of
statements of economic interests filed by nonjudicial candidates
for county office?

CONCLUSION

Copies of statements of economic interests filed by candidates
for nonjudicial county office are retained by the county clerk.

ANALYSIS

Candidates for specific elected offices are required to file
statements of economic interests. (Sections 87200 and 87201.)
The filing official who receives candidates' statements is

_________________________

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Linda D. Hamilton
Page 2

identified by the statute for each type of office sought.
(Section 87500.) For instance, candidates for the offices of
state legislator and state Board of Equalization are required to
file statements of economic interests with the officer who
receives their declarations of candidacy. (Section 87500(d).)
Candidates for county boards of supervisors are required to file
statements of economic interests with the clerk of the county in
which they are elected. (Section 87500(f).)

The only exception to precise identification of the filing
official is for candidates for judicial office. (Section
87500(i).) Because the statute contains no reference to
candidates for judicial office, the previous advice letter to you
(Hamilton, supra) recommended that copies of statements of
economic interests filed by nonincumbent candidates for judicial
office should be retained by the clerk of the court. That advice
is pending statutory change which may remove any ambiguity
regarding who is the proper filing official.

In your most recent letter, dated June 27, 1988, you ask for
clarification as to the proper filing official for nonjudicial
candidates. A filing official retains copies of candidate
statements of economic interests.2/ The summary shown below
identifies the filing official for each category of candidate
filer which you listed in the enclosure with your letter.

FILING OFFICIALS FOR COUNTY 721 FILERS

Where to File

| Category of filer | Code section | Incumbents | Nonincumbent candidates only |
|---|---|---|---|
| Superior court judges and court commissioners and candidates | 87500(i) | clerk of court | clerk of court (see FPPC Advice letter No. A-88-141) |
| Chief administrative officers DAs and candidates County counsel and candidates Supervisors and candidates | 87500(e) | county clerk | county clerk |
| County planning commissioners | 87500(g) | own agency | not applicable |

2/A "filing official" makes and retains a copy of a disclosure statement but does not retain the original statement. Originals are forwarded to the FPPC, which is the filing officer.

Linda D. Hamilton
Page 3


If you have further questions, please call me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

[handwritten signature]
By: Bruce W. Robeck
Political Reform Consultant

SUPERIOR COURT/COUNTY CLERK                                    COUNTY OF SAN BERNARDINO

☒ 351 N. Arrowhead Ave., Fifth Floor, San Bernardino, CA 92415-0210 • (714) 387-3921
☐ West District, 8303 Haven Avenue, Rancho Cucamonga, CA 91730 • (714) 945-4136
☐ Desert District, 14455 Civic Drive, Victorville, CA 92392 • (619) 243-8670
☐ North Desert District, 235 E. Mountain View Avenue, Barstow, CA 92311 • (619) 256-4816


June 27, 1988


Ms. Lilly Spitz
Counsel, Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
PO Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95804-0807

Re:  Request for Advice
     Your File No. A-88-141

Dear Ms. Spitz:

     We need further clarification on your response to the above
request.  Please refer to the attached copy of my April 6th
letter.  I was and am now again requesting clarification as
to the filing of all candidates' statements.  The Registrar
of Voters forwarded to us the candidate statements for all
elected offices (see attached list and memo).

     As you know, the declarations of candidacy are filed with
the Registrar of Voters and the Form 721's as indicated in
my April 6th letter are filed with this office.  But, we
desire verification as to whether the candidate statements,
Forms 721, (see attached Final Candidate List for 1988
filings) should be filed with us, the Registrar of Voters,
or some other department.

     We are requesting a letter of intrepretation as to the
above.

     If you require further information, please contact me at
(714) 387-3970.

Sincerely,

[signature]

Linda D. Hamilton
Secretary/Deputy County Clerk

SUPERIOR COURT/COUNTY CLERK                                    COUNTY OF SAN BERNARDINO

☒ 361 No. Arrowhead Ave., Fifth Floor, San Bernardino, CA 92415-0210 • (714) 387-3821
☐ West District, 8303 Haven Avenue, Rancho Cucamonga, CA 91730 • (714) 945-4136
☐ Desert District, 14455 Civic Drive, Victorville, CA 92392 • (619) 243-8670
☐ North Desert District, 235 E. Mountain View Avenue, Barstow, CA 92311 • (619) 256-4816

April 6, 1988


Mr. Bruce Robeck
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95804

Dear Mr. Robeck:

     Pursuant to our telephone conversation of April 1, 1988,
we are requesting a Letter of Interpretation for guidelines
as to where Candidate Statements' Forms 721's are to be
filed in San Bernardino County.

     The Registrar receives all other campaign/election documents
but feels that the Forms 721's are to be filed with the
County Clerk's Office.  Enclosed is a copy of a transmittal
memo from the Registrar of Voters to the Technical
Assistance and Analysis Division on this subject.

     The County Clerk's Office receives Forms 721 for the
Superior Court Judges, Commissioners, CAO, County Counsel,
District Attorney, Board of Supervisors and Planning
Commissioners.  After a review of our records for the past 3
years, we found no record of Candidate Statements being
received or filed in our office.

     Therefore, a Letter of Interpretation is requested at this
time.

     If you require further information, please contact me at
(714) 387-3970.

Sincerely,

Linda D. Hamilton
Secretary/Deputy County Clerk

cc:Audrey Butler, Deputy Registrar

REGISTRAR OF VOTERS                                                                                  COUNTY OF SAN BERNARI
                                                                                                               GENERAL SERVICES AGEN
777 East Rialto Avenue • San Bernardino, CA 92415-0770 • (714) 387-8300
                                                                                                               GERANE B. JACOBSEN
                                                                                                               Registrar of Voters
                                                                                                               F. P. HESTON
                                                                                                               Assistant Registrar of Vot


                              March 28, 1988




TO:    Technical Assistance and Analysis Division

FROM:  REGISTRAR OF VOTERS, SAN BERNARDINO COUNTY
       Audrey Butler, Deputy Registrar

RE:    Candidates' Statements of Economic Interests (Form 721)

Confirming my telephone call with your office on March 14, and your
reply that information would be forthcoming in about a week with your
Memo on the handling of Form 721s. Your memo was received on
March 21 and we are forwarding 34 copies of Candidates' Form 721.

Your information that documents be date stamped was received after
the fact; also, inasmuch as the County Clerk is the filing officer,
we are also sending a copy there to be filed.

       Please call A. Butler, 714/387-2082, if you have any questions or
if we can be of further service.

statements of economic interests filed by nonjudicial candidates 
for county office?

CONCLUSION: Copies of statements of economic interests filed by candidates 
for nonjudicial county office are retained by the county clerk.
